title: Exposing native code through JavaScript interfaces is security-sensitive
description: Exposing native code to JavaScript in WebViews can introduce severe security risks, such as allowing malicious JavaScript to access sensitive device features or data. This rule detects when native code is exposed to JavaScript interfaces in Android WebViews, which should be avoided unless absolutely necessary and properly secured.
recommendation: Avoid exposing native code to JavaScript in WebViews. If exposure is necessary, ensure that only trusted content is loaded and that all exposed methods are secured against misuse.
impact: Potential for arbitrary code execution or data leakage if malicious JavaScript gains access to native interfaces.

compliant:
// Do not expose native interfaces unless absolutely necessary
// If you must, validate all input and restrict access to trusted content only

checks.message: Avoid exposing native code to JavaScript in WebViews. If exposure is necessary, ensure that only trusted content is loaded and that all exposed methods are secured against misuse.
